title: Severity catalogue
description: The 4 severity levels + their SLAs, notification routing, and post-incident requirements. MonitoringPagingCoordinator reads this to route alerts to providers. EscalateIncidentJob reads this for the escalation SLA. MonitoringIncidentObserver reads this for the postmortem trigger.

severities.description: Complete outage or critical degradation affecting production. Every fire pages the primary on-call via SMS + phone + push + email. Escalates to backup on-call after 5min without ack. Auto-triggers postmortem workflow on resolve.
example_scenarios: Payment provider integration failing all requests

severities.description: Significant degradation affecting a subset of production. Pages primary on-call via push + email + mail. Escalates after 15min without ack. Retrospective encouraged but not required.
provider_routing: paging providers (PagerDuty, Opsgenie) — if configured with p2 in enabled_severities

severities.description: Minor degradation or leading indicator. In-app notification only. No paging. No escalation. Reviewed during next business hours.
provider_routing: APM providers (Sentry) — if configured with p3 in enabled_severities
example_scenarios: P95 latency > 500ms (leading indicator, not yet page-worthy)

severities.description: Informational signal — dashboard-only. No notifications. No escalation. Used for weak signals, trends, and health-check-persistently-failing warnings.
provider_routing: APM providers — if configured with p4 in enabled_severities (rare — usually filtered)
